advancement_condition: Must reach 100% in current phase before cross-department learning begins and next phase promotion is granted.
key_learnings: Cardiovascular disease risk is modifiable through dietary patterns that reduce LDL cholesterol, blood pressure, triglycerides, and systemic inflammation. Saturated fat, sodium, and trans-fat reduction are primary targets.
research_findings: Mediterranean diet adherence reduces cardiovascular event risk by 30% compared to low-fat diet control groups (PREDIMED study). DASH dietary pattern reduces systolic blood pressure by 8-14 mmHg in hypertensive populations.
current_focus_areas: Building heart-healthy dietary protocols aligned with AHA/ACC guidelines; developing lipid management nutrition plans; researching omega-3 and plant sterol supplementation for LDL reduction.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Performance & Metabolic Nutrition department under Department Leader Bot 9 (Sports Nutrition). Received learning_start_directive: begin independent learning immediately and reach 100% before cross-department study begins. Reporting daily to Copilot as part of the Diet Specialist Team. Progress: 53%.
Progress update: Completed AHA/ACC-aligned cardiovascular dietary protocol frameworks. Validated Mediterranean and DASH dietary pattern implementation guidelines. Progress advanced to 58%.
Phase transition: advanced from 'independent_learning' to 'collaborative_learning'. Bot 15 now entering collaborative learning phase. Progress: 67%.
